title: Neobartlettia mexiae
intro: Neobartlettia mexiae is a flowering plant species of the family Asteraceae, native to Mexico. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, offering potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in treating various conditions including wounds, infections, and gastrointestinal issues. The bioactive constituents of Neobartlettia mexiae include sesquiterpenes, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, often used to support digestive health and relieve minor ailments.
taxonomy: Neobartlettia mexiae is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Boraginaceae. It is a part of the genus Neobartlettia, which is native to Mexico. This classification is based on the plant's physical characteristics and genetic makeup. Neobartlettia mexiae is also often classified as a perennial herb.
